question: What are the admission requirements for undergraduate programs?
answer: Applicants must have completed high school with a minimum 60% aggregate. SAT or equivalent test scores are required for international students.

question: When does the admission process start?
answer: The admission process typically starts in June and continues until August for the Fall semester.

question: How can I apply for admission?
answer: You can apply online through our university portal. Fill out the application form and upload required documents.

question: What documents are needed for admission?
answer: Required documents include: high school transcripts

question: Is there an application fee?
answer: Yes
category: there is a non-refundable application fee of PKR 2000 for local students and $50 for international students.

question: What is the deadline for Fall admissions?
answer: The deadline for Fall semester admissions is typically August 15th. Late applications may be considered on a case-by-case basis.

question: Can I apply for multiple programs?
answer: Yes
category: you can apply for up to two programs in a single application cycle.

question: What is the minimum GPA requirement?
answer: The minimum GPA requirement is 2.5 on a 4.0 scale or 60% aggregate marks.

question: Are scholarships available?
answer: Yes
category: we offer merit-based and need-based scholarships. Applications are reviewed automatically during admission.

question: How do I check my admission status?
answer: You can check your admission status by logging into the applicant portal using your application ID.

question: What programs are offered at the university?
answer: We offer undergraduate and graduate programs in Engineering

question: Is there a Computer Science program?
answer: Yes
column5: and PhD in Computer Science programs.

question: What is the duration of undergraduate programs?
answer: Undergraduate programs typically take 4 years (8 semesters) to complete.

question: Are evening classes available?
answer: Yes
category: we offer evening programs for working professionals in selected disciplines.

question: Can I transfer credits from another university?
answer: Yes
category: credit transfer is possible subject to evaluation by the academic department. Maximum 50% credits can be transferred.

question: What is the fee structure?
answer: Fee structure varies by program. Undergraduate programs range from PKR 150000 to PKR 250000 per semester.

question: Are installment plans available for fee payment?
answer: Yes
category: fees can be paid in two installments per semester with prior approval from the finance office.

question: Is financial aid available?
answer: Yes
category: we offer financial aid to deserving students. You must submit income documents and apply through the financial aid office.

question: What payment methods are accepted?
answer: We accept bank deposits
column4: and credit/debit card payments through our portal.

question: Are there any additional charges?
answer: Additional charges may include lab fees
column4: and examination fees depending on your program.

question: Where is the university located?
answer: The main campus is located in Karachi
column4: with additional campuses in Lahore and Islamabad.

question: What facilities are available on campus?
answer: Campus facilities include libraries

question: Is hostel accommodation available?
answer: Yes
category: separate hostel facilities are available for male and female students on a first-come-first-served basis.

question: How is campus security?
answer: The campus has 24/7 security with CCTV surveillance

question: Is there a library on campus?
answer: Yes
category: we have a central library with over 100000 books

question: What is the student-faculty ratio?
answer: Our student-faculty ratio is approximately 20:1
category: ensuring personalized attention and quality education.

question: Are there sports facilities?
answer: Yes

question: Is there a medical facility on campus?
answer: Yes
category: we have a medical center with qualified doctors and nurses available during university hours.

question: Can I visit the campus before applying?
answer: Yes
category: campus tours are available on weekdays. Please contact the admissions office to schedule a visit.

question: Is parking available for students?
answer: Yes
category: parking facilities are available for students with registered vehicles.

question: When does the academic year start?
answer: The academic year consists of Fall (September) and Spring (February) semesters.

question: What is the attendance policy?
answer: Students must maintain at least 75% attendance in each course to be eligible for final examinations.

question: How is the grading system?
answer: We follow a GPA system with grades from A to F. A=4.0

question: Can I drop a course after registration?
answer: Yes
category: courses can be dropped within the first two weeks of the semester without academic penalty.

question: What happens if I fail a course?
answer: Failed courses must be repeated. You can retake the course in the next semester when it is offered.

question: Are online classes available?
answer: Yes
category: we offer hybrid learning with some courses available online

question: How do I register for courses?
answer: Course registration is done online through the student portal during the designated registration period.

question: What is the exam schedule?
answer: Mid-term exams are held in the middle of the semester
category: and final exams are conducted at the end.

question: Can I take extra courses?
answer: Yes
category: you can take additional courses with approval from your academic advisor

question: What is academic probation?
answer: Students with GPA below 2.0 are placed on academic probation and must improve their performance.

question: How do I contact the admissions office?
answer: You can email admissions@university.edu or call +92-21-99261261 during office hours.

question: What are the office hours?
answer: The admissions office is open Monday to Friday

question: Is there a helpline for queries?
answer: Yes
category: our helpline +92-21-99261262 is available for student queries during office hours.

question: How can I get a transcript?
answer: Transcripts can be requested through the student portal or by visiting the registrar office.

question: Who do I contact for technical issues?
answer: For technical issues with the portal

question: How do I report a complaint?
answer: Complaints can be submitted through the student portal or directly to the student affairs office.

question: Is there an alumni network?
answer: Yes
category: we have an active alumni network. You can join after graduation through the alumni office.

question: How do I get a degree verification letter?
answer: Degree verification letters can be requested from the registrar office with a processing time of 3-5 days.

question: Can international students apply?
answer: Yes
category: we welcome international students. Additional documentation and visa requirements apply.

question: What are the English proficiency requirements?
answer: International students must provide IELTS (6.0+) or TOEFL (80+) scores unless from English-speaking countries.
